schema_notes.energy_delta: Positive replenishes energy, negative spends it.
schema_notes.stress_delta: Negative reduces stress; positive increases pressure.
schema_notes.money_delta: Positive gains money; negative spends it.
schema_notes.social_delta: Positive improves social fulfillment or relationship progress.
schema_notes.hygiene_delta: Positive improves cleanliness and self-care status.
schema_notes.discipline_delta: Positive improves routine, consistency, or self-control progress.

motives.label: Survival
motives.description: Maintain food, water, sleep, hygiene, and shelter.

motives.label: Health
motives.description: Improve physical wellness, energy, recovery, and resilience.

motives.label: Mental Stability
motives.description: Reduce stress and keep mood, focus, and emotional balance steady.

motives.label: Comfort
motives.description: Make daily life feel safer, cleaner, calmer, and more pleasant.

motives.label: Routine
motives.description: Build repeatable habits and a reliable day-to-day structure.

motives.label: Discipline
motives.description: Become more consistent, controlled, and dependable over time.

motives.label: Productivity
motives.description: Get more done with less friction and less wasted time.

motives.label: Mastery
motives.description: Improve skills through repetition, study, and deliberate practice.

motives.label: Education
motives.description: Learn formally or informally to expand knowledge and options.

motives.label: Career
motives.description: Advance work status, competence, reputation, and opportunity.

motives.label: Wealth
motives.description: Accumulate money, assets, savings, and financial security.

motives.label: Stability
motives.description: Create a dependable life with fewer emergencies and disruptions.

motives.label: Independence
motives.description: Rely less on others for money, housing, transport, and decisions.

motives.label: Belonging
motives.description: Feel connected to family, friends, groups, and community.

motives.label: Love
motives.description: Build affection, trust, intimacy, and lasting partnership.

motives.label: Friendship
motives.description: Create and maintain supportive reciprocal social bonds.

motives.label: Family
motives.description: Strengthen family relationships, care, and shared responsibility.

motives.label: Reputation
motives.description: Be seen as reliable, kind, skilled, attractive, or respectable.

motives.label: Service
motives.description: Help others and contribute in practical or emotional ways.

motives.label: Community
motives.description: Become part of a larger social fabric and shared place.

motives.label: Meaning
motives.description: Feel that life and effort matter beyond short-term tasks.

motives.label: Identity
motives.description: Become the kind of person the character wants to be.

motives.label: Self Expression
motives.description: Communicate personality, taste, feelings, and creativity.

motives.label: Creativity
motives.description: Make original things and develop artistic or imaginative ability.

motives.label: Fun
motives.description: Seek enjoyment, amusement, novelty, and playful relief.

motives.label: Restoration
motives.description: Recover from fatigue, burnout, conflict, or illness.

motives.label: Security
motives.description: Reduce risk, danger, debt, scarcity, and uncertainty.

motives.label: Order
motives.description: Create cleanliness, structure, predictability, and control.

motives.label: Control
motives.description: Feel agency over schedule, body, space, and future.

motives.label: Freedom
motives.description: Open more choices in time, place, money, and lifestyle.

motives.label: Status
motives.description: Gain recognition, influence, prestige, or visible success.

motives.label: Romance
motives.description: Pursue attraction, dating, intimacy, and commitment.

motives.label: Parenting
motives.description: Care for children or dependents and shape their wellbeing.

motives.label: Homebuilding
motives.description: Create a stable, functional, and welcoming living space.

motives.label: Mobility
motives.description: Improve movement, commuting, travel, and geographic access.

motives.label: Preparedness
motives.description: Be ready for tomorrow, setbacks, and opportunities.

motives.label: Healing
motives.description: Repair damage from grief, trauma, conflict, or failure.

motives.label: Confidence
motives.description: Grow courage, social ease, and trust in one’s own ability.

motives.label: Spirituality
motives.description: Seek peace, faith, reflection, reverence, or inner alignment.

motives.label: Legacy
motives.description: Leave a lasting impact, body of work, or remembered contribution.

motives.label: Adventure
motives.description: Experience exploration, surprise, challenge, and new places.

motives.label: Pleasure
motives.description: Seek sensory satisfaction, beauty, relaxation, and delight.

motives.label: Longevity
motives.description: Build habits that support a longer, healthier life arc.

motives.label: Balance
motives.description: Keep work, health, relationships, and rest in sustainable proportion.

motives.label: Self Respect
motives.description: Live in a way the character can admire and stand behind.

motives.label: Resilience
motives.description: Bounce back after setbacks and keep functioning under pressure.

motives.label: Ownership
motives.description: Acquire and maintain property, tools, collections, or territory.

motives.label: Recognition
motives.description: Be noticed, appreciated, thanked, or publicly validated.

motives.label: Transformation
motives.description: Make a major life change and become someone different.

motives.label: Fulfillment
motives.description: Reach a life that feels complete, worthwhile, and deeply satisfying.

actions.label: Have trouble getting out of bed
Start the day in a bad mood
Lose track of time in the morning
Forget an essential item at home
Leave the house without a wallet
Leave the house without a phone
Wear the wrong outfit for the weather
Use too much soap or shampoo
Feel too tired to get ready
Eat junk food out of stress
Realize you’re out of an ingredient
Feel shaky from low blood sugar
Get stopped for a minor issue
Fail to find a parking spot
End up waiting in line too long
Make a room dirtier while cleaning
Forget to take out the trash
Realize the light bulb is out
Break a vacuum or cleaning tool
Open the wrong tab or file
Make a simple mistake at work
Get the wrong tone in a message
Start too many tasks at once
Hear a weird noise in the house
Create more mess while fixing something
Wake up with a dry mouth
Ignore a small problem until it grows
Make a poor decision while tired
Make a poor decision while stressed
End the day feeling like nothing got done

Aliveness / Joy.notes: Amplifies rewarding, expressive, and celebratory actions.

Accepting / Open.notes: Supports regulated, grounding, and recovery-oriented actions.

Angry / Annoyed.notes: Can intensify conflict and impulsive release while harming cooperation.

Connected / Loving.notes: Improves bonding, support, and intimacy-related actions.

Courageous / Powerful.notes: Boosts decisive, effortful, and challenge-facing actions.

Curious.notes: Supports exploration, discovery, and novelty-seeking actions.

Despair / Sad.notes: Pushes inward toward recovery and away from demanding or exposing actions.

Disconnected / Numb.notes: Flattens engagement and favors low-effort disengaged actions.

Embarrassed / Shame.notes: Can increase self-protective maintenance while reducing exposed actions.

Fear.notes: Increases avoidance and caution while hurting exposed or uncertain actions.

Fragile.notes: Needs buffering, safety, and low-pressure recovery actions.

Grateful.notes: Stabilizes generous, healthy, and connective actions.

Guilt.notes: Can drive repair, apology, and making-things-right behaviors.

Powerless.notes: Suppresses agency-heavy actions and favors passive ones.

Stressed / Tense.notes: Makes relief-seeking more attractive while reducing efficiency of effortful actions.

Tender.notes: Supports nurturing, intimate, and emotionally open actions.

Unsettled / Doubt.notes: Can lean toward checking, planning, and hesitation-heavy actions.
